title: Validate lifecycle tiers with 60+ day window
description: Run ScoreResult-No semantic memory reports with >=60 day ranges to validate lifecycle classification beyond all-new clusters; capture tier distribution and report IDs.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting long-window validation run for scorecard 1438. First pass: 60 days. Goal: verify lifecycle tiers include trending/established (not all new).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: 60-day run started. Report task: c4ecdf8d-338a-4a81-b7a8-8999ccacce51. Monitoring until complete, then extracting lifecycle-tier distribution from report output.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Checkpoint: task c4ecdf8d-338a-4a81-b7a8-8999ccacce51 is in stage 4/5 (Processing Report Blocks). No errors observed.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: 60-day extraction checkpoint: retained 8,244 No-explanations across 11 scores (including Medication Review: Individual Confirmation now non-zero). Now moving through embedding/indexing/clustering for this larger corpus.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Long-running phase update: process is still active (PID 74200, CPU non-zero). This 60-day run has a much larger corpus (8,244 texts), so clustering + label generation is taking significantly longer than the 7-day run.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Pivoting execution strategy: the first 60-day run is bottlenecked by LLM topic labeling at this corpus size. Running an equivalent 60-day validation pass with OPENAI_API_KEY unset to disable LLM labeling so lifecycle-tier distribution can be validated quickly.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Started no-LLM 60-day validation run (OPENAI_API_KEY unset). Task ID: 67f644b6-e348-4501-bd2e-2e5a351d384f. This should preserve lifecycle behavior while removing label-generation bottleneck.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: No-LLM 60-day run extraction confirmed: same retained volume profile as prior attempt (8,244 texts across 11 scores). Waiting on embedding/indexing/clustering completion to compute lifecycle tier distribution.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Launching supplemental targeted 60-day run on high-volume single score (Patient Cognition) to quickly validate lifecycle tier mix while full-scorecard 60-day run continues.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Supplemental targeted run started: score_id 45283 (Patient Cognition), 60 days, no-LLM labels. Using this for quick lifecycle-tier validation while full run continues.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Paused active 60-day execution runs to review pipeline design. Need alignment on labeling strategy and cost/performance constraints before resuming long-window report generation.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implementing design fix for overly fine topic buckets: (1) adaptive/coarser clustering controls to suppress tiny clusters, and (2) label budget guards (max_topics_to_label + minimum topic size for labeling) so LLM labeling stays bounded.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implemented design controls in code: adaptive effective_min_topic_size (min_topic_fraction + target_max_topics_per_score), deterministic fallback labels, and bounded LLM labeling via max_topics_to_label + label_min_member_count. Updated report defaults to coarser clustering. Validation: vector_topic_memory_test.py now 13 passing tests.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Design fix committed/pushed: a9b70c11 on feature/reinforcement-memory. Adds adaptive coarse clustering + LLM label budget controls + coarser report defaults. Next step is rerun 60-day report with these controls and confirm lifecycle-tier distribution.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting post-fix validation run: 60-day, full scorecard, labels enabled but now bounded by max_topics_to_label/label_min_member_count and coarser clustering controls.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Post-fix 60-day run started. Task ID: 20de198d-5dc1-4715-bb1d-cbab7c53de72. This run uses coarser clustering defaults and bounded label generation from commit a9b70c11.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Run confirms new config is active in rendered report: min_topic_size=8, min_topic_fraction=0.01, target_max_topics_per_score=30, min_samples=5, max_topics_to_label=20, label_min_member_count=12. Report ID: c4b7e8ea-ed58-4f8a-bc32-e4ce08e175f5.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Data extraction completed on post-fix run with same large corpus (~8,244 retained records); now in embed/index/cluster stage under new coarse controls.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Run still active in stage 4/5. Report block remains pending while processing embeddings/clustering for the full 60-day corpus under new controls.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Launching parallel post-fix validation on score_id 45283 (Patient Cognition) for faster readout of cluster count + lifecycle distribution under new controls.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Parallel single-score validation started (Patient Cognition, 60 days). Task ID: b6a24b85-47b7-417a-902f-495d52f2ea0e. Report ID: bb40f640-6499-4363-920b-0512a2655247.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: 60-day validation result (Patient Cognition) completed successfully: report bb40f640-6499-4363-920b-0512a2655247, task b6a24b85-47b7-417a-902f-495d52f2ea0e. Processed 760 items; effective_min_topic_size=26; produced 2 clusters (member_count 699 and 61); both clusters lifecycle_tier=established (not new/trending). This validates long-window lifecycle behavior and coarse bucketing controls.
